tool_args.code:
import subprocess
import sys

try:
    result = subprocess.run(
        [sys.executable, "/a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py"],
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
        timeout=300
    )
    print(f"STDOUT:\n{result.stdout}")
    print(f"STDERR:\n{result.stderr}")
    print(f"Return code: {result.returncode}")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error executing backup script: {e}")

/a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py 397 lines
>>>
 1 #!/usr/bin/env python3
 2 """
 3 Git Backup Script
 4 
 5 Backs up specified paths to their configured git remotes.
 6 Prioritizes local changes over remote while preserving remote history.
 7 """
 8 
 9 import subprocess
10 import argparse
11 import json
12 import sys
13 from datetime import datetime
14 from pathlib import Path
15 
16 # ============================================================================
17 # CONFIGURATION - Edit this list to change backup paths
18 # ============================================================================
19 BACKUP_PATHS = [
20     "/a0/usr",
21 ]
22 # ============================================================================
23 
24 
25 def run_command(cmd, cwd, dry_run=False, check=True):
26     """Run a shell command and return the result."""
27     if dry_run:
28         print(f"  [DRY-RUN] Would run: {' '.join(cmd)}")
29         return subprocess.CompletedProcess(cmd, 0, b"", b"")
30     
31     result = subprocess.run(
32         cmd,
33         cwd=cwd,
34         capture_output=True,
35         text=True
36     )
37     
38     if check and result.returncode != 0:
39         raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(
40             result.returncode, cmd, result.stdout, result.stderr
41         )
42     
43     return result
44 
45 
46 def is_git_repo(path):
47     """Check if a directory is a git repository."""
48     git_dir = Path(path) / ".git"
49     return git_dir.exists()
50

tool_args.code:
import subprocess
import sys

notification_msg = "✅ Git Backup Successful\n\n📦 Backed up: /a0/usr\n🔧 Branch: main\n📤 Status: Successfully pushed to origin/main\n\nResults: 1 succeeded, 0 failed"

result = subprocess.run([
    sys.executable,
    "/a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py",
    notification_msg,
    "--title", "Git Backup Result",
    "--priority", "3"
], capture_output=True, text=True)

print(f"Notification STDOUT:\n{result.stdout}")
print(f"Notification STDERR:\n{result.stderr}")
print(f"Return code: {result.returncode}")

Execute the git-backup skill by running the backup script at /a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py, then use the notify-user skill to send a notification with the backup result.
